content.problemStatement:
Let's call a string XXX interesting if it satisfies the following conditions:


XXX contains at least KKK pairwise distinct characters
for any two characters c1c_1c1‚Äã and c2c_2c2‚Äã which appear in XXX, the frequency (number of occurrences) of c1c_1c1‚Äã in XXX is equal to the frequency of c2c_2c2‚Äã in XXX
You are given a string SSS. Compute the number of interesting substrings of SSS. Two substrings are considered different if they are located at different positions in SSS.
constraints: the sum of ‚à£S‚à£|S|‚à£S‚à£ in all test cases does not exceed 14,00014,00014,000
the sum of ‚à£S‚à£|S|‚à£S‚à£ in all test cases does not exceed 2,0002,0002,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a string SSS, followed by a space and and integer KKK.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the number of interesting substrings.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The interesting substrings are "ab", "ba", "ab", "bc", "abab" and "abc".
